title: Example of a Service‑Based Work Breakdown Structure for Wedding Planning
summary: Illustrates a service‑oriented Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) for a wedding, showing main deliverables such as Attire, Ceremony, Reception, and optional services, and explains how removing optional items automatically updates schedule and budget.
content: Level 1 includes Attire, Ceremony, Reception, Rehearsal Dinner, Bridal Lunch, Accommodations, and Transportation. Each level can contain sub‑deliverables (e.g., Ceremony → Minister). Optional services may be omitted, causing the WBS to recalculate scope, schedule, and cost. The example emphasizes the need to include people‑related deliverables when they are part of the service.
can_answer: How is a service‑oriented Work Breakdown Structure organized for a wedding project?
warnings: Leaving out a required person (e.g., Minister) can cause last‑minute scope changes.
